# Shape Rotator Journal

Version: public content v0.0.1

This file lists the current journal articles and their raw Markdown sources for LLM retrieval, copy/paste, and citation.

## Full Context Synthesis

The dominant hidden theme across almost every Shape Rotator cohort project is not simply AI apps, TEEs, or agent tooling. The deeper recurring layer is coordination under partial trust.

The cohort is collectively exploring: How do humans, agents, workflows, and organizations coordinate effectively when context, trust, privacy, and memory are all fragmented?

Core mechanisms:

- preserving agency while increasing automation
- routing information safely
- memory persistence
- local-first and sovereign systems
- human-agent collaboration
- reducing invisible coordination overhead
- collaboration without full disclosure
- context fragmentation
- making invisible work legible
- privacy-preserving intelligence systems

Project/topic anchors:

- TinyCloud
- TSQL
- Conclave
- Router
- Smithers
- SignalSext
- anonymous credential systems
- workflow routing systems
- AI orchestration systems

- https://os-web.shaperotator.xyz/workspace/journal/articles/llm-agents-memory-workflows-social-routing

- https://os-web.shaperotator.xyz/workspace/journal/articles/private-ai-capability
- https://os-web.shaperotator.xyz/workspace/journal/articles/private-ai-capability/article.md

## Topic map

- LLM agents
- AI workflow memory
- agentic systems
- human-in-the-loop AI
- human override
- private AI
- AI privacy
- trusted execution environments
- sensitive context
- AI capability
